textByHashes.9724ff272f6c202b58af4a72ce3b6194869eda25: I see. Then I will come at once. Where shall we meet?
textByHashes.3a8919510b5e02e6b18db8fb91873647fcd2d354: Very well. I will join you there soon.
textByHashes.e5c6f398900a42175b54c1944ce440daf860e34b: I will join you at Xeric's Lookout soon.
textByHashes.bab832b67c1dfc9a0014638ffeb79caaafc124fa: Rose? You speak of the King's daughter. What happened?
textByHashes.2df58b89d6aa36e67051e6e0eef08b05f06d42a2: If the Council are to be removed, they will also have to be replaced.
textByHashes.ba90c84c073a90e24e6446a7494609c7e75944ab: A new Royal Accord would require all five us to agree on the contents.
textByHashes.cd0837f9fa2164ec61898425b7473ae6a158ed3f: A break might be beneficial to all of us. We have a lot to consider.
textByHashes.c470e0860ad5f95efd809cd6e5ec3a82a1215586: Hello there. I was hoping you'd come and speak with me. There is much we need to discuss.
textByHashes.36e1bbb7045c4d998e188235c5a2e8565309aa93: Not exactly. That revelation is concerning and needs to be addressed. However, I fear we may be facing a much larger issue.
textByHashes.7ec966af3a0b92af25315d436d89ed6b133ed9f1: When we spoke before, you mentioned that all of this had some connection to the death in Arceuus. Tell me, does that have anything to do with the mage you mentioned?
textByHashes.bb6c7ffa722e9c1b6275988f5d5de1453185bedd: I see. And did this Xamphur look similar to an Arceuus citizen?
textByHashes.67550b91731ec983c589ac99a0a31b1320242b01: Xamphur is not a common name, and yet it is one I have heard before. During the Age of Strife, Xeric himself was assisted by a loyal servant of that same name.
textByHashes.e343f90b65ad1f9da53024f0c7262162c8d1a12f: Once, we would never have considered it. However, a recent discovery you made yourself means it's a very real possibility.
textByHashes.bac629c221df77c2ad511d4a913a15c2acd7b727: Yes. Thanks to that altar, Xeric and his followers had access to the same power that keeps every citizen of Arceuus alive.
textByHashes.17099b203cb36780d741d15fe0db6e8a4c2af329: With that power, they may well have lived much longer than originally believed. It's possible one of them even lives to this day.
textByHashes.30956b5a5b4c8702843ce0625a6a05835cbbc714: Hmm. This is interesting and concerning. Perhaps this client is the one Xamphur considers his master.
textByHashes.4062df9d1811eaf430b915d9fb3e995d3c8b9ffa: Be careful, friend. Empty speculation will be of little help to us here.
textByHashes.b26912fd43c5038463d8c3356c8f19dd342240fe: Of course. However, if you consider one option too closely, you'll become blind to all other possibilities. What we need here is proof.
textByHashes.3d964c12ea991152e87d3a320d744b207e76b840: My friend, I must ask that you once again to pay a visit to the Tasakaal at Mount Karuulm. With their power, they will be able to determine if this Xamphur is the same one that served Xeric.
textByHashes.165f8bc4e5b135988c0c686cbb1678e071b7396e: The power of the Dark Altar leaves traces. If Xamphur's soul inhabits a body created using the Dark Altar, the Tasakaal will be able to determine when that body was formed.
textByHashes.43e4de728deb143bdfc10323495cbfc7e9194e98: Ideally, but I suspect this will also work.
textByHashes.7063ebe5829ca758f81f76d553e5a94e5dd042ac: Since you destroyed it, we've been researching this device to try and determine the origin. Thanks to you, we now know that it was made by Xamphur.
textByHashes.dd14c496066bf2819d05272b1011f2a6fd392b09: The Council must be removed and a replacement is needed. I will gladly support the new Royal Accord. But first, we must focus on Xamphur.
textByHashes.9a8cb32fb561af46fe5ec45712d3844f490d89cf: Hello there. Have you spoken with the Tasakaal?
textByHashes.5be703d85ee2edb96f1e2520b26dfb8224c74edb: Please do so soon. Only they have the power to determine who Xamphur is.
textByHashes.9fa9308b6c80114c261d5e21f617eb710bdfd4d2: So it's as I feared. This is not good.
textByHashes.5430f81e4dac69e7f874915762233bc6adfa7b68: A Pyromancer? Do you know who?
textByHashes.2310a012e3d3eaf06bbd80f9c87f0c4110dd1627: Are you sure about this? Had the Doors of Dinh fully failed, the Wintertodt would have destroyed Kourend. What would Xamphur gain from that?
textByHashes.a58eccd5edae2a884c94e3a8bbc205a7c8b7a152: I will have someone look into this. We'll also use this new knowledge to try and locate Xamphur.
textByHashes.060976c4bba3491bfa4d9bab4e0cc99982e23b43: Interesting. This is useful information that could help our search. I wonder... could they mean the southern Kingdom of Varlamore?
textByHashes.8febaa5a1bcf16172fc188462ec1295a57fcf1ea: Ah, but that's one for another time. I believe we've learnt all we can about Xamphur for now. Until we can locate him, we must turn our focus towards the Council.
textByHashes.f74311a7c6faa1e6ff91524843eca4486b8c88ee: I will. While I have no desire to rule myself, I'll gladly support one of my fellow leaders in that role.
textByHashes.3f010b8da9229e561792a79694a9c51d5a6959ac: Hello there. You have my support with the Royal Accord. You should speak with the others.
textByHashes.aac244889d18d2363b8fb0501d9307918a40d5de: Lord Hosidius requested a private word with Lord Shayzien outside. I'm sure they'll be here soon.
textByHashes.06a7f0090005925912b95fc542b4557c983a5b7a: In fact, I think I hear them now.
textByHashes.7d826722b6176c95dca3cf9c4b1ce9f91cc7ed6e: My position is a unique one. As such, I feel it would be inappropriate for me to involve myself too heavily in this. That said, I will gladly support the decision made by this group.
textByHashes.af1a728c8b4b52bd6245b7e83ec7af13226286e1: I will gladly advise the next ruler in any way I can, but it should not be me who takes the throne. I would recommend you look to Lord Hosidius or Lord Shayzien for that.
textByHashes.a9c01b3dbdf6ba430200b7f310d9db236c15214b: I would be happy to see the people have a say.
textByHashes.7de03b6a434a0670f717fb318a0fb47d338042dc: Lord Hosidius would be a fitting choice for the role, assuming no one else here is willing.
textByHashes.160eed6dd88e63dc7177141a8d279fd102fabd2e: Hello there. It's time for us to deal with the Council. You should speak with Commander Fullore.
textByHashes.7da7fcaa6deda855898900f56b0d6a7c2913563e: Nothing yet. However, we did manage to search his lair in Molch.
textByHashes.6b6eb02929db98ae68c67c56f95de4f169a7aedb: Most of it had been cleared out before we arrived. There was some research left behind though. Notes on potential new spells.
textByHashes.c1eb69690e5c4d7b631dda9a198465b222336dca: Some are far too dangerous to use, but others we've found to be safe. We're preparing a tome of all the ones that are suitable. I'll make sure you get a copy once we're done.
textByHashes.0b250d59ba290519e47743bff2c6bcfe286747ae: It's the least we can do after all you have done for us. Anyway, I won't keep you. Enjoy the coronation.
textByHashes.018f7b119240e1ec1b7d32ad5d35e1bba4c1a43f: You're the one that saved our people. On behalf of all of Arceuus, I thank you.
textByHashes.224b369d47e12daedf6111a994904101d3e0aeee: It seems so. A short while ago I felt the power of the Dark Altar return to normal levels. I was safely able to stop my ritual and let the Altar work on its own again.
textByHashes.aa56431c1473e0b3f3c2c0786f3c5ffeae121f8a: Now tell me, what was the cause of this and how did you stop it?
textByHashes.cbd0472718846604426f0d6fdaabc25259047b6c: Well that's no surprise. The Tasakaal seem to feel that only they deserve immortality. It's rather hypocritical of them. It clearly didn't stop them helping you though.
textByHashes.407c11ce6c3330a177afaafc3b2e9774d03df134: Such is their way. They act superior to us, but the reality is they'll work with anyone if they think it will further their aims.
textByHashes.11fd1b7bea4849ca9225c0f56e8292f92e731263: So how did they help you?
textByHashes.cfae19e9a9123d1df5c6e5238e121bfd959cfd78: The Tasakaal view everyone who isn't one of them as a mere mortal, even those of us who have also achieved immortality. That doesn't narrow down the options very much.
textByHashes.562a25db48e7ef187e3ee3cff4351e9df481b786: This is most concerning though. If someone deliberately sabotaged the Altar, who knows what else they might do. I'll have someone recover the remains of this device. Maybe we can work out where it came from.
textByHashes.c6663a676c2004ded84c06100af9e23b3132ae2d: For now though, I'd like to once again thank you for your help. Today was a dark day for our people but without you it would have been our end. Please accept these gifts. They are the least you deserve.
textByHashes.031af8f44dd343dd45400faa86b2076d5bba6156: Now, can I help you further?
textByHashes.02aeb63c228075bdc1b334e9651e626d2329d78e: I am Trobin Arceuus, lord of Arceuus. It's a position I've held for a thousand years, ever since the Ascent of Arceuus.
textByHashes.9a5f6c2fd146726886706223cedeae16a43b1af5: Indeed. I've been present at some of the most notable moments in Kourend's history.
textByHashes.7a309e2eef69b705a71142d78d6bda6f833aba7d: I was there when King Agata IV was deposed, I was there when Kharedst the Builder opened Kourend Castle and I was there when Emperor Imafore was executed.
textByHashes.fcec98c29afc6cb5be0dacc445007295a21b6048: I tend to keep out of public affairs these days though. My presence is often more of a distraction than a benefit. It also means I can focus on my work in peace.
textByHashes.625cbf6f40edbecac3f2bd076f524ae4e3d8e081: The freeing of souls. There are many souls that find themselves trapped here after death. My people and I do our best to release them.
textByHashes.c97b0481215f9ccb5d510fddde42cc1b3ce126f0: If you find something magical in Kourend, chances are it came from Arceuus. Our people have lived for a thousand years. The knowledge we've gained in that time has resulted in many a magical discovery.
textByHashes.778357e1a0086cbe878dade97719dd707c8a17f4: Most of our discoveries start with the Dark Altar. It is the source of our immortality and our people dedicate their lives to studying it.
textByHashes.f17a1788f61c0fc7ae214b42ed18450c0f9263fd: Our people also serves an important role in the wider world. We work to free trapped souls so that they may pass on to the next world. It is a crucial task that the Dark Altar helps us to perform.
textByHashes.954896da19876c231390a75746e2d1ee32671b08: Impossible to say. The Dark Altar has been mentioned in records dating back to the earliest days of Kourend. Before then, who knows.
textByHashes.f9dafb1b26cd884df662d60a09a62e7c622a8286: No. The Ascent of Arceuus didn't occur until after Xeric's Age of Strife, hundreds of years after Kourend was formed.
textByHashes.53851ab0e6fc6c4076b106c874f75265d52f4e3c: It was a thousand years ago, during the time of King Byrne II. I went to my people and shared the secrets of immortality with them.
textByHashes.c81dea59c41506c4d113d9b0818c62991326bb4a: Not all of them accepted this gift of course, as was their right. Those that did, were granted new bodies to house their souls. These bodies are sustained by the Dark Altar.
textByHashes.90c4c7e2bc70b9e24acb4ade3ce4c0f7969d7338: No. It was granted to us by the Tasakaal. They are ancient beings, ones that make us seem like mere children.
textByHashes.67219652749e51e5274f1fe11bacda2c323a172f: Their age and experience have granted them knowledge over life and death that we can only dream of.
textByHashes.3a1b071beeb11387db845e40773ce6a405edd9f3: The events that led to the Ascent of Arceuus are more complicated than even the Tasakaal know. But that is a story for another day.
textByHashes.5b6c7eebc42aa7d94dd914067513d8df3ad0dec8: I had one of my people recover the remains of that device. The one that was absorbing the Dark Altars power.
textByHashes.c6de60085d67bf44349f0e69da60fb378bdcfb96: We're performing tests to see if we can learn more about it. We haven't made any meaningful progress yet though.
textByHashes.dbf25a27e681d18fa7b4f97f822be9a499c1839b: Once we've learnt more about it, we'll be in a better position to determine our next move.
textByHashes.3b4a447a785b78ef4acee0a27c803365bbd68f39: I admit, the idea of someone sabotaging the Altar is more than a bit concerning. I hope we can make some progress soon.
textByHashes.36d37bbb1bbac1724567b728ed8ea5bbbff21e2b: Now that we know Xamphur was responsible for the device that was absorbing power from the Dark Altar, we're hoping we can use it to help locate him.
textByHashes.22c15a1992a37e5cecaecb87dad1786ac549cc8f: Alas, so far we've had no luck. We're also scanning for energy that matches what we observed during that earthquake he caused, but again, nothing has been discovered so far.
textByHashes.c8d5e23e7672370d2b768a53a47df9e943a03f16: It seems this work will take time. I assure you though, we will find him and his master.
textByHashes.32cce9ad46cd96405f6263d445d79258452ea51c: We've begun an investigation into their order, but we have little to go on. Hopefully we find a good lead soon.
textByHashes.777bb78f9fbc6631793861af34306a263dcb3d9e: Thank you for helping our people. Please accept these gifts, they are the least you deserve.
